analysis_result: Log-rank test p-value for S462TY sonidegib vs vehicle = 0.027 (rounds to 0.03, matching the paper).
denominator_used: N = 10 mice (5 S462TY-Vehicle + 5 S462TY-Sonidegib). All 10 events observed (no censoring).
assumptions_made: All survival times in Fig 5l represent endpoint events (no censoring indicator in the source sheet), so event_observed = 1 for every mouse; the typo 'Sonedigib-5' is treated as the 5th sonidegib mouse; two-sided log-rank test via lifelines.statistics.logrank_test.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM10_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Fig 5l'.
   - 20 rows x 2 columns (sample ID, Survival (days)).
   - Two xenograft models (S462TY, STS-26T) x two arms (Vehicle, Sonidegib) x 5 mice each.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter to sample IDs beginning with 'S462TY-' (dropping STS-26T rows).
   - S462TY-Vehicle: 5 mice (survival days = 52, 41, 57, 34, 45).
   - S462TY-Sonidegib: 5 mice (survival days = 81, 40, 78, 85, 70). The sheet contains a typo 'Sonedigib-5' which is treated as the 5th sonidegib mouse.
   - Final N = 10.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Duration: Survival (days) column.
   - Event indicator: set to 1 for all mice (standard convention for preclinical xenograft survival experiments when no censoring column is provided).

D. COMPUTATION
   - Statistical method: log-rank test via lifelines.statistics.logrank_test.
   - Call: logrank_test(durations_A=vehicle_days, durations_B=sonidegib_days, event_observed_A=ones(5), event_observed_B=ones(5)).
   - Test statistic: 4.8958.
   - p-value: 0.026922.

E. RESULT
   - log-rank p = 0.027 (reported to 2 decimal places as p = 0.03, consistent with the paper's stated p = 0.03).

F. CAVEATS
   - Small sample size (n = 5 per arm); log-rank test has limited power and approximate chi-square distribution with small N.
   - All events assumed observed (no censoring). If any mouse was euthanized for non-tumor reasons or still alive at study end, the p-value would change slightly.
   - Typo 'Sonedigib' in the source sheet treated as 'Sonidegib' (the 5th sonidegib mouse).
